UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the LocalListings application. This entity stores data necessary for identity and interaction, excluding sensitive authentication credentials handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's unique email address, used for authentication (via an external system) and communication. (Must be verified)

displayName.description: The public-facing name chosen by the user, displayed on their listings and profile.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture, typically displayed in the top right corner of the UI.

location.description: The user's general geographical location (e.g., city, state) for local search and context.

isEmailVerified.description: Flag indicating whether the user's email address has been successfully verified (e.g., via OTP).

registeredAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Defines a category or subcategory used to organize and browse classified listings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

description.description: A brief explanation of what type of listings belong to this category.

parentCategoryId.description: Reference to a parent category's ID if this is a subcategory. Null for top-level categories. (Relationship: Category 1:N Category)

Listing.title: Listing
Listing.description: Represents a single classified advertisement posted by a user, including details, photos, and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Listing entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created and owns this listing. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Listing)

title.description: The concise title of the classified ad, potentially generated or refined by the AI Listing Assistant.

description.description: A detailed description of the item or service being advertised, potentially refined by the AI Listing Assistant.

price.description: The asking price for the item or service, in numerical format.

condition.description: The physical condition of the item (e.g., 'New', 'Used - Like New', 'Used - Good', 'Used - Fair').

location.description: The specific geographical location (e.g., street address, city, postal code) where the item is located or service is offered.

categoryIds.description: An array of IDs referencing the categories this listing belongs to. (Relationship: Category N:N Listing)

photoUrls.description: An array of URLs pointing to images associated with the listing.

status.description: The current status of the listing (e.g., 'Active', 'Sold', 'Pending', 'Draft', 'Archived').

postedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the listing was initially published.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the listing's details were modified.

viewsCount.description: A numerical count of how many times this listing has been viewed by users.

Conversation.title: Conversation
Conversation.description: Represents a private messaging thread between a listing owner and an interested user about a specific listing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Conversation entity.

listingId.description: Reference to the Listing that this conversation pertains to. (Relationship: Listing 1:N Conversation)

listingOwnerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns the listing and is a participant in this conversation. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Conversation)

inquirerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated the inquiry and is a participant in this conversation. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Conversation)

startedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the conversation was first created.

lastMessageAt.description: Timestamp of the most recent message sent within this conversation, useful for sorting conversations.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a single message sent within an in-app conversation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the Conversation this message belongs to. (Relationship: Conversation 1:N Message)

senderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who sent this message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Message)

content.description: The actual text content of the message.

sentAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the message was sent.

isRead.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the recipient of the message has read it.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile information. Access is private, restricted to the user whose ID matches `userId`. The path `userId` corresponds to `request.auth.uid` for ownership. Other users may have limited read access to specific public fields (e.g., `displayName`, `profileImageUrl`) through explicit rules.

Defines global categories for listings. All documents in this collection have a homogeneous security posture: publicly readable by all users (authenticated or not). Write access is restricted to administrators (not defined in entities but implied for managed data).

Stores individual classified advertisements. Includes denormalized `userId` field to establish direct ownership and enable authorization independence. The `status` field (e.g., 'Active', 'Draft') dictates public vs. private visibility. Publicly active listings are readable by all; private/draft listings are restricted to the owner (`userId`). Rules will enforce `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` or `resource.data.status == 'Active'`.

Represents a private messaging thread between two users about a specific listing. Includes denormalized `listingOwnerId` and `inquirerId` for authorization independence. Also includes a `participants: string[]` array (containing both `listingOwnerId` and `inquirerId`) to enable efficient queries for all conversations involving a specific user via `array-contains` and to simplify security rules. Access is restricted to users present in the `participants` array.

Represents a single message within a conversation. For authorization independence and to avoid `get()` calls to the parent `Conversation` document, this document denormalizes the `participants: string[]` array (or `listingOwnerId` and `inquirerId`) from its parent `Conversation`. Access is restricted to users present in this denormalized `participants` array.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for LocalListings is designed with a strong emphasis on authorization independence, debuggability, and efficient querying, adhering to the specified core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (Mandate A):** This is achieved primarily through strategic denormalization. For documents requiring access control based on parent attributes or multiple participants, the necessary authorization context (e.g., owner IDs, participant lists) is copied directly into the document itself. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, ensuring atomic operations and simplifying rule logic. Specifically:
- **`Listing` documents:** Include `userId` to directly identify the owner, allowing rules to authorize based on `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without looking up the `UserProfile`.
- **`Conversation` documents:** Include `listingOwnerId` and `inquirerId` as primary participants. Furthermore, a `participants: string[]` array is added to denormalize all involved user IDs, simplifying rules for participant-based access and client queries.
- **`Message` documents:** Crucially, `Message` documents, nested under `Conversation`, also denormalize the `participants: string[]` array (or `listingOwnerId` and `inquirerId`) from their parent `Conversation`. This is critical to ensure that a `Message`'s authorization can be determined solely from its own data, making rules robust and independent of the parent `Conversation` document's existence or state when evaluating `read`/`write` for messages.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This structure supports secure `list` operations by ensuring that client queries include the necessary filters that align with the security rules. 
- **`UserProfile`:** Path-based (`/users/{userId}`) naturally supports QAPs, as a user queries their specific, private profile.
- **`Category`:** Globally readable, so `list` operations are open.
- **`Listing`:** The `/listings/{listingId}` collection handles both public (`status: 'Active'`) and private (`status: 'Draft'`) listings. QAPs are maintained because clients must issue specific queries: public users will query with `where('status', '==', 'Active')`, while listing owners will query with `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. Rules will then verify these conditions on the `resource.data`.
- **`Conversation`:** The `participants` array enables efficient and secure `list` queries for a user's conversations using `array-contains(request.auth.uid)` without violating QAPs. Any conversation returned by this query will contain the requesting user's UID in its `participants` list.
- **`Message`:** Messages are accessed via `/conversations/{conversationId}/messages/{messageId}`. Since `Message` documents contain the `participants` array, rules can directly check `resource.data.participants.includes(request.auth.uid)`, allowing secure `list` operations within a specific conversation for authorized participants.

**Structural Segregation (Mandate B):** While `Listing` uses a `status` field within a single collection for practicality (avoiding data migration on status change), it's handled safely with client-side query enforcement and server-side rule validation. Other entities maintain homogenous security postures per collection (e.g., `/users` is private, `/categories` is public, `/conversations` is participant-specific).

**Access Modeling (Mandate C):** 
- **Private Data:** `/users/{userId}` for `UserProfile` adheres to path-based ownership.
- **Hierarchical/Nested Data:** `/conversations/{conversationId}/messages/{messageId}` follows the recommended hierarchy.
- **Collaborative Data:** `Conversation` and `Message` utilize the `participants` array for robust collaborative access control.

**Data Clarity (Mandate D):** Semantic naming (`userId`, `listingId`, `conversationId`, `messageId`) and an explicit `status` field for `Listing` contribute to predictable schemas and debuggable rules.
